identity.title: Adversarially re-evaluate and remediate the compatibility inventory and corpus

authorization.reference: Operator requested evaluation tests on WP-FF-004 through different adversarial lenses, remediation of findings, gaps, concerns, and risks, and code cleanup where needed.

lifecycle.next_action: Commit the completed adversarial evidence, run FF-GATE-PR-001 from the clean closure commit, then record MERGE_PENDING proof.

scope.summary: Re-prove the validated WP-FF-004 v1 contract through fresh independent attack lenses, remediate every reproduced false-PASS, security, attribution, and robustness defect, simplify brittle code, and rerun the complete compatibility and canonical gate matrix.
in_scope: Preserve and freshly re-prove every WP-FF-004 v1 acceptance requirement
Attack pinned profile/corpus/live-manifest integrity and completeness rather than trusting self-consistent counts
Attack replay and differential producer-consumer boundaries, candidate attribution, classification integrity, and report truthfulness
Attack sanitizer, path containment, live URL policy, malformed input, duplicate argument, and failure-report behavior
Add focused regression tests and negative fixtures for confirmed findings
Remove or simplify brittle, duplicated, misleading, or unnecessary code only while full scope remains proven
Run canonical compatibility commands, FF-GATE-ARCH-001, FF-GATE-PR-001, counterfactuals, boundary probes, and negative paths
non_goals: Ferric extractor, transport, archive, JavaScript, FFmpeg, or shipped runtime implementation
A replacement-parity, performance, release, or broad Phase 1 readiness claim
Changing the pinned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 baseline or adding a production Python/yt-dlp dependency
Adding a new runtime, native dependency, worktree, or delegated agent
dependencies: WP-FF-004 v1 validated implementation and six acceptance requirements
Pinned Rust toolchain, Cargo, Git, and existing locked dependencies
Pinned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 profile, corpus, oracle manifest, and separately acquired oracle inputs when regeneration is exercised
No new product runtime or native dependency

acceptance_matrix.requirement: All six WP-FF-004 v1 acceptance requirements remain preserved and are freshly re-proven against current source, manifests, fixtures, reports, manual, and canonical commands.
evidence: All six v1 acceptance requirements were re-read and preserved.

Clean compatibility validation, full replay, 2/4/0/1 deterministic shards, empty-candidate diff, identity inventory diff, 43 Rust tests, warnings-denied Clippy, and architecture-check pass at 9a39cc597da1fc646c628e64910dbfcf2a0f947e.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Pinned-content integrity, replay/differential truthfulness, sanitization/path/live-network security, and Rust failure-path/cleanup lenses each derive concrete attack surfaces and execute checks beyond v1 evidence, including at least two counterfactuals, one producer-consumer boundary probe, and one negative-path probe.
evidence: Eleven structure-aware tests were first observed RED and then GREEN across all four declared lenses.

Independent command probes proved Windows CRLF digest failure, differential bypass of failing canonical validation, missing candidate attribution, canonical corpus rejection, and junction containment.

Four concrete counterfactuals, two producer-consumer boundary probes, and malformed/oversized/omission/classification/security negative paths are recorded under extensions.change_evidence.adversarial_review.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Every reproduced finding, gap, concern, and risk is remediated with executable regression proof or retained as explicit bounded residual uncertainty that does not contradict the Phase 0 proof ceiling; no known false-PASS route remains.
evidence: Every confirmed finding is recorded under extensions.change_evidence.independent_findings with executable remediation.
The exact 16-mutation compatibility validator and 24 compatibility-focused tests pass.
Residual uncertainty is bounded under extensions.change_evidence.residual_uncertainty and does not contradict the Phase 0 proof ceiling.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: A reuse, deletion, dependency, and simplification review removes or improves brittle or misleading implementation where safe, and the complete accepted scope/risk matrix passes again after cleanup.
evidence: Mutation handling was split into profile, oracle, corpus, and candidate helpers; candidate validation/classification and generic inventory deltas removed duplicated long paths.
No dependency, runtime, crate, feature, or parallel framework was added.
Formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, 43 tests, compatibility commands, and architecture-check passed after simplification.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, the full Rust test suite, deterministic compatibility validation/replay/diff/inventory-diff probes, FF-GATE-ARCH-001, FF-GATE-PR-001, governance parsing, authority mirror, allowlist, registry-target, and git diff checks pass on the final clean branch with truthful proof limitations.
evidence: FF-GATE-PR-001 is the remaining final clean aggregate proof.

checks_run: Authority, topology, build rules, packet template, v1 packet/refinement, cited design anchors, and readiness gates read
git rev-parse HEAD and git merge-base main HEAD: f94c17854213fff4dbd82e754314f37a011db3a3
Integrated WP-FF-004 diff and complete compatibility.rs implementation inspected
Baseline 28 tests PASS but compatibility-validate FAIL on all seven CRLF-converted case digests
Eleven independent structured mutations reproduced false-PASS or policy-bypass behavior before remediation
After remediation: formatting PASS; warnings-denied Clippy PASS; 43 tests PASS
Clean compatibility validate/replay/shards/diff/inventory-diff and FF-GATE-ARCH-001 PASS at 9a39cc597da1fc646c628e64910dbfcf2a0f947e

last_checkpoint.next_action: Commit completed evidence and rerun FF-GATE-PR-001 from the clean closure commit.

checks: 24 compatibility-focused tests and 43 full Rust tests PASS
Compatibility validation PASS with seven planes and exact 16 required negative mutations
Full and 2/4/0/1 sharded replay PASS with explicit per-case receipts
Candidate-attributed differential report PASS for completeness with seven explicit missing_feature rows
Inventory-diff v2 PASS across options, presets, interactions, extractors, and extractor descriptions
Canonical containment and corrupted-corpus command probes PASS by exact rejection
not_proven: Any Ferric feature behavior or replacement parity
Exhaustive behavioral coverage for every inventory row or extractor
Cross-host oracle generation or live-site stability
Adversarial closure until all v2 acceptance rows pass

changed_requirements: Executable reproduction and remediation of confirmed findings
Post-remediation cleanup and complete proof rerun

required_outcomes: Make pinned inventory and corpus omissions or rewrites fail closed
Keep replay and differential reports complete, attributable, and truthful
Keep offline validation network-free and live canaries strictly bounded and non-proof
Reject secrets, machine-local paths, malformed identities, duplicates, and mismatched inputs
risk_cases: Self-consistent profile or corpus mutation bypasses validation
Diff or replay reports PASS without validating or attributing consumed inputs
Live report or URL policy overstates proof or permits unintended destinations
Secret or machine-local value bypasses a narrow scanner
Malformed, duplicate, unknown, or partial input is ignored
Cleanup removes a required compatibility or proof surface

review_gates.scope_and_risk_coverage: PASS: four attack lenses, eleven structure-aware RED-to-GREEN tests, two command boundary probes, four counterfactuals, canonical report inspection, and exact negative paths cover the declared WP-004 v2 risk matrix.
review_gates.reuse_deletion_dependency_simplification: PASS: remediation reuses strict Serde, SHA-256, atomic reports, source attribution, deterministic collections, std filesystem canonicalization, and the existing mutation runner; no dependency or parallel framework was added.
review_gates.post_simplification_complete_rerun: PASS: formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, 43 tests, compatibility validation/replay/shards/diff/inventory-diff, and architecture-check are green after refactoring long mutation, classification, and inventory-delta paths.

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: no mechanism beyond the accepted outcome
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: rejected for reproduced false-PASS and security paths that require executable remediation; retained for speculative cases that did not reproduce
selected for strict Serde schemas, SHA-256 helpers, atomic reports, source attribution, and existing mutation fixtures
selected for deterministic collections, path canonicalization, bounded parsing, and focused unit tests
selected for Git, filesystem, and separately acquired pinned oracle execution where required
existing serde, serde_json, and sha2 are sufficient; no addition is currently justified
selected private integrity, containment, classification, sanitization, and inventory-delta helpers plus four new production-linked negative fixtures and focused tests

artifact_specific_justification.xtask: The reviewed generator, validator, replay, diff, sanitization, and report code lives in the existing non-shipped compatibility module.
artifact_specific_justification.fixtures: Pinned manifests, cases, candidates, and mutation fixtures are the producer-consumer inputs under attack.
artifact_specific_justification.model_manual: Any changed command, limitation, diagnostic, or recovery behavior must remain operable by a no-context model.
artifact_specific_justification.governance: The v2 packet, taskboard focus, and missing v1/v2 durable IDs are minimum authorization, recovery, and registry state.

change_evidence.pre_simplification_verdict: FAIL: baseline unit tests were green while canonical validation failed on Windows, and eleven structured mutations plus command probes exposed integrity, attribution, truth-state, classification, sanitizer, path, negative-inventory, resource-bound, and inventory-coverage gaps.
simplification_changes: Split mutation execution into profile, oracle, corpus, and candidate helpers instead of extending one long match body.
Separated candidate input validation from per-row classification and decision authorization.
Replaced five repeated inventory map/diff blocks with one generic typed inventory_delta helper.
Centralized canonical JSON content digests, LF-normalized fixture hashing, bounded JSON reads, and canonical path containment.
Kept all remediation inside the existing non-shipped compatibility module and existing serde/serde_json/sha2/std surface.
change_evidence.post_simplification_verdict: PASS: the refactored module preserves the complete v1 contract, closes every reproduced path, adds no dependency or runtime surface, and passes warnings-denied Clippy plus the complete accepted scope/risk matrix.
complete_post_change_verification: cargo clippy --manifest-path build/Cargo.toml --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked --target-dir build/target -- -D warnings: PASS
cargo test --manifest-path build/Cargo.toml --workspace --all-targets --all-features --locked --no-fail-fast --target-dir build/target -- --test-threads=1: PASS, 43 tests
compatibility-validate: PASS, seven planes and exact 16 negative mutations
compatibility-replay full and shards 0/4 through 3/4: PASS, exact 2/4/0/1 distribution with explicit per-case checks
compatibility-diff empty candidate: PASS for completeness, seven missing_feature rows and candidate SHA-256 attribution
compatibility-inventory-diff identity comparison: PASS with ff.compatibility-inventory-diff@2 and all five row families
canonical junction containment probe: PASS by exact rejection before parse
mutated canonical corpus through compatibility-diff: PASS by FF-COMP-E-CORPUS-INTEGRITY rejection
cargo run --manifest-path build/Cargo.toml --locked -p fforager-xtask -- architecture-check: PASS

ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.current_ceiling: The pinned inventory and representative seven-plane corpus are Phase 0 oracle infrastructure; they do not prove Ferric feature parity, exhaustive extractor behavior, cross-host generation, or live-site stability.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.replacement_trigger: Ferric candidate producers, a new pinned baseline, supported-host expansion, or broader corpus coverage activates additional versioned cases and differential proof.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.likely_upgrade_path: Extend the same stable IDs, strict schemas, pinned integrity, classified differences, deterministic fixtures, and explicit live-observation separation.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.trigger_detection_method: Profile/corpus IDs, pinned hashes, active packet acceptance, supported-host policy, candidate manifests, and inventory-diff reports fail closed.

DIFF_ATTACK_SURFACES: Generated profile and corpus validators versus self-consistent row/count/digest mutations
Canonical profile/corpus producers versus replay, differential, and inventory-diff consumers
Candidate classification and decision authorization versus truthful report output
Committed fixture sanitization, machine-local path detection, and placeholder allowlisting
Lexical repository paths versus canonical filesystem containment and reparse points
Live-canary initial destinations and OBSERVED-only truth state
Negative-fixture inventory completeness, malformed/oversized JSON, and bounded resource behavior
Inventory-diff coverage across every versioned row family
INDEPENDENT_CHECKS_RUN: Ran compatibility-validate on a clean Windows checkout after all 28 inherited tests passed; it failed all case digests because core.autocrlf converted LF to CRLF.
Ran compatibility-diff while canonical compatibility-validate was failing; it emitted PASS and omitted the candidate input hash before remediation.
Executed eleven semantically valid in-memory mutations that reached deep validators rather than shallow parse failures; all were RED before remediation and GREEN after.
Inspected final validate/replay/diff/inventory reports directly for exact negative IDs, per-case replay checks, candidate attribution, missing rows, and schema version.
COUNTERFACTUAL_CHECKS: If validate_current_profile_content in build/tools/fforager-xtask/src/compatibility.rs were removed, a pinned option row and its count could be removed together without failing validate_profile.
If validate_corpus_without_files were removed from differential_rows, compatibility-diff could again emit PASS for a self-consistently corrupted canonical corpus.
If require_safe_input used only safe_relative and prefix checks, a build/target junction could route candidate reads into .GOV.
If base_report hardcoded status PASS, compatibility-live-canaries would again overstate nondeterministic OBSERVED rows as deterministic gate success.
BOUNDARY_PROBES: Producer-consumer probe: temporarily changed a canonical corpus expected_outcome and proved compatibility-diff rejects it with FF-COMP-E-CORPUS-INTEGRITY before report emission; mutation was reverted.
Filesystem probe: created a verified temporary build/target junction to .GOV and proved candidate resolution rejects canonical containment before parsing; the junction was removed.
Report probe: final compatibility-diff input map contains candidate:build/fixtures/compatibility/candidate-empty-v1.json with its SHA-256 and seven explicit missing_feature rows.
NEGATIVE_PATH_CHECKS: Invalid canonical profile passed to differential and inventory diff
Invented stable divergence decision and nondeterministic classification on a deterministic case
Missing one of the exact required negative fixtures
Private live destination and live OBSERVED report status
X-Api-Key, access_token, unknown placeholder, Windows forward-slash, macOS, and UNC path bypasses
Oversized JSON input bounded at 16 MiB
CRLF fixture digest equivalence and canonical junction escape
adversarial_review.INDEPENDENT_FINDINGS: See extensions.change_evidence.independent_findings; every confirmed finding is remediated and regression-tested.
adversarial_review.RESIDUAL_UNCERTAINTY: See extensions.change_evidence.residual_uncertainty; remaining risks are bounded and do not raise the Phase 0 proof ceiling.

independent_findings: REMEDIATED HIGH: all inherited unit tests passed while Windows core.autocrlf made every canonical case digest fail; fixture integrity now hashes UTF-8 after CRLF/CR to LF normalization and has a portability regression test.

REMEDIATED HIGH: profile validation trusted self-consistent counts and digest shapes; canonical serialized content digests now bind the oracle manifest, profile, corpus, and live manifest to their versioned IDs.
REMEDIATED HIGH: corpus expected outcomes and fixture schema/case identity were not enforced; canonical corpus integrity plus per-plane schema and case-ID checks now fail closed.
REMEDIATED HIGH: compatibility-diff bypassed canonical profile/corpus validation and could emit PASS while compatibility-validate failed; diff now validates every canonical input and fixture first.
REMEDIATED MEDIUM: differential reports omitted the candidate path/hash; the candidate is now canonically contained, bounded, parsed, and attributed by SHA-256.

REMEDIATED HIGH: arbitrary syntactically stable IDs authorized accepted divergences and deterministic offline cases could be labeled nondeterministic; decision allowlisting and deterministic classification constraints now reject both paths.
REMEDIATED HIGH: live-canary reports hardcoded PASS and accepted any HTTPS destination; reports now emit OBSERVED and manifest rows must match exact public destination allowlists and pinned content.

REMEDIATED HIGH: sanitizer checks missed common sensitive-key aliases, unknown placeholders, forward-slash Windows paths, macOS paths, UNC paths, URL userinfo, and additional assignment forms; explicit key/placeholder/path/assignment allowlists and regression tables now cover them.

REMEDIATED HIGH: compatibility input/output checks were lexical and vulnerable to link/reparse escape; canonical containment under build/fixtures/compatibility or build/target is now required and a real junction probe proves rejection.
REMEDIATED MEDIUM: JSON reads were unbounded; the shared reader now caps every compatibility JSON input at 16 MiB and a sparse oversized probe proves bounded rejection.
REMEDIATED HIGH: deleting up to five negative fixtures retained PASS because only a minimum of seven was required; validation now requires the exact 16-ID mutation inventory.
REMEDIATED MEDIUM: replay receipts exposed only a count and shard; each selected case now emits its stable ID, plane, expected outcome, and fixture digest.

REMEDIATED MEDIUM: inventory diff accepted changed content under one profile ID and ignored presets, interactions, and descriptions; schema v2 rejects same-ID drift and reports all five versioned row families.

REMEDIATED QUALITY: extended long functions triggered warnings-denied Clippy; mutation domains, candidate classification, and inventory deltas were separated into reusable private helpers with no lint allowance or new dependency.
REMEDIATED GOVERNANCE: WP-004 v1 refinement/ceiling and v2 packet/refinement/ceiling durable IDs were missing from the registry; all six targets are now registered.
residual_uncertainty: Pinned canonical digests intentionally make any legitimate profile, corpus, or live-manifest content change require a new version/ID and reviewed digest update; this is a fail-closed maintenance cost, not automatic baseline migration.

Exact initial live URL allowlists do not prove safety of DNS changes or redirects performed inside the external yt-dlp oracle; live execution remains explicit opt-in, nondeterministic, credential-free, and excluded from acceptance proof.

Canonical path checks occur before later file operations and do not provide handle-based protection against a hostile local actor racing filesystem links after validation; the current Phase 0 threat model records but does not claim elimination of that local TOCTOU class.

The sanitizer is a finite allowlist backed by pinned current fixtures and regression cases; it cannot prove detection of every future secret format, so new fixture schemas or credential forms must extend the allowlist and negative corpus.
Cross-host oracle regeneration, live-site stability, exhaustive extractor behavior, and any Ferric feature parity remain unproven.
